name: TFS — Monthly 12-Month Rollover + Dead Letter Log
description_for_humans: Two small but important hygiene workflows bundled in one file. Build as separate n8n workflows using the node groups below.

workflow_a_rollover.name: TFS — Monthly Deals Rollover
workflow_a_rollover.purpose: Decrement Deals Last 12 Months for buyers whose most recent close was over 12 months ago. Keeps tier scoring accurate.
workflow_a_rollover.cron_human: Runs at 9 AM on the 1st of every month (Phoenix time)
logic: 1. GET all contacts with tag buyer:active AND Deals Last 12 Months > 0
2. For each contact, GET opportunity history in Buyer Lifecycle pipeline — filter to 'Closed' stage
3. Count opportunities closed in the last 365 days → that's the true deals_last_12_months
4. If count differs from the stored field value, PUT update to correct it
5. If count drops below the tier threshold (3), also trigger a re-scoring webhook to WF01 Step 5 logic
workflow_a_rollover.cost_note: Runs once a month. Self-hosted n8n = $0. Negligible API load on GHL — expected ~200 contacts checked, well under rate limit.

workflow_b_dead_letter.name: TFS — Match Engine Dead Letter Log
workflow_b_dead_letter.purpose: Catches any match engine execution that errors or produces zero matches. Logs to a Notion database for manual review. Without this you have no visibility when a deal quietly doesn't go out.
workflow_b_dead_letter.trigger: Add an Error Trigger node to the main match engine workflow (01_buyer_match_engine.json). Route all errors here. Also add a conditional branch in the main workflow — if matched_count === 0, also send to this logger.
workflow_b_dead_letter.action: Write a row to Notion database 'TFS — Match Engine Log' with fields: deal_id, source, asset_class, market, price, matched_count, error_message, timestamp, reviewed (checkbox default false)
workflow_b_dead_letter.weekly_review_sop: Every Monday morning, open the 'TFS — Match Engine Log' Notion view filtered to Reviewed=false. Any deal with 0 matches means either (a) the buyer list doesn't have coverage for that market/asset combo — use as a signal to recruit buyers, or (b) the deal's fields are misconfigured and the deal needs fixing. Check off Reviewed when addressed.
